title: Lightly Fermented Thai Pork Skewers
description: A central Thai dish featuring yuzu and robata grilled pork, lightly fermented for a crunchy texture, this recipe is egg free friendly and perfect for a snack or dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together yuzu juice, fish sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and Thai chili to make the marinade.
Add the sliced pork to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for 2 hours to lightly ferment.
Soak the bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread 2-3 pieces of the marinated pork onto each skewer, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill with vegetable oil to prevent sticking, then grill the skewers for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Season the skewers with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with chopped cilantro or scallions if desired.
